fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 65 uses `now > effective` (strictly greater than), but the specification requires that an entry is expired when `now >= effective` (i.e., when `now` equals the expiry it should also be considered expired). The boundary case where `now == effective` incorrectly returns the cached value instead of None.

constraints.intent: At the boundary where now equals effective, the condition should treat the entry as expired; the strict > allows equal timestamps to pass through incorrectly.
After the else branch (entry not returned as None), now must be strictly less than effective; equality should have caused expiry.
The effective expiry should be at least as large as expires_at, confirming _effective_expiry adds non-negative tolerance; verifies effective__1 is correctly computed before the comparison.
The function should only return a non-None value when now is strictly less than the effective expiry.
